LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALMA NOVOA-EVERSOLE'S MOTION TO CONTEST THE APPLICATION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT (BETWEEN PLAINTIFFS AND MS. ELIZON'S INSURER) IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO FILING OF A GOOD FAITH MOTION WHICH IS BASED ON SUFFICIENT FACTS ESTABLISHING THE ABSENCE OF ANY ASSETS ATTRIBUTABLE TO MS. ELIZON THAT COULD BE PAID IN SETTLEMENT OF THIS LAWSUIT. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. COUNSEL FOR MS. NOVA-EVERSOLE IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND SUBMIT IT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-08-2018 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest The Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ALMA NOVOA-EVERSOLE'S Motion To Contest The Application For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement (between plaintiffs and Ms. Elizon's insurer) is GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE to filing of a good faith motion which is based on sufficient facts establishing the absence of any assets attributable to Ms. Elizon that could be paid in settlement of this lawsuit. The papers filed on behalf of Ms. Elizon do not adequately show that there are no assets attributable to Ms. Elizon that could be paid in settlement of this lawsuit. Nor do those papers show that sufficient efforts were made to locate any heirs of Ms. Elizon or determine the scope of her decedent's estate.

Counsel for Ms. Nova-Eversole is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-25-2017 Set For Trial On Sep-04-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
